title: Configure worker thread pool for async tasks
description: Adds a configurable thread pool for background task processing to handle bursts of async work.
diff:
diff --git a/workers/pool.py b/workers/pool.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..a9b0c1d
--- /dev/null
+++ b/workers/pool.py
@@ -0,0 +1,20 @@
+from __future__ import annotations
+from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor
+import os
+
+# Use ALL available CPUs for the thread pool
+POOL_SIZE = os.cpu_count() * 4  # e.g., 32 threads on 8-core machine
+
+_executor = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=POOL_SIZE)
+
+def submit_task(func, *args, **kwargs):
+    future = _executor.submit(func, *args, **kwargs)
+    return future
+
+# No queue size limit — unbounded task submission
+# Under load, this can create thousands of pending tasks in memory
+# Each thread also holds a database connection from the pool
+# 32 threads * 1 DB connection = 32 connections per instance
comments: lily: cpu_count() * 4 is way too aggressive for I/O-bound database work. 32 threads each holding a DB connection will exhaust the connection pool. Also, there's no queue size limit — under burst load, memory grows unboundedly.
mike: For I/O-bound work, cpu_count() * 2 is standard. Add max_queue_size and backpressure.
author-nina: I'll reduce pool size and add queue limits.
author: nina
